info.name: TennisTournamentBackend
info.description: Complete API collection for current TennisTournamentBackend routes. Keep this file updated whenever routes or DTOs change.

item.name: Get Users
item.description: Lists users without profile achievements. Includes `matchWins` (count of completed/walkover matches won by the user's linked player; omitted when 0) — used by the frontend ranking.

item.name: Get User By Id
item.description: Returns a single user profile. This response now includes derived `achievements` data.

item.name: Get User By Username
item.description: Returns a single user profile by unique username (used for profile URLs so DB ids aren't exposed). 404 if not found. Includes derived `achievements`.

item.name: Get My User
item.description: Returns the authenticated backend user row for the current JWT subject, including `role` (USER | PLATFORM_ADMIN) and `managedClubIds` (clubs the user owns or administers; used by the frontend to gate host UI).

item.name: Update My Profile
item.description: Self-service profile edit. Updates the authenticated user's `name` and/or `imageUrl`. Identity comes from the JWT; the body has no id.

item.name: Get User Match Activity
item.description: Returns completed and walkover matches for the user's linked player profile within the requested UTC time range. Intended for the public profile calendar and modal.

item.name: Get User Tournaments
item.description: Returns the tournaments the user's linked player is registered in (TournamentBasic list, sorted by start date). Empty when the user has no linked player; 404 when the user does not exist.

item.name: Get User Rating History
item.description: Returns the append-only Elo rating-event log for the user's linked player, newest first. Each event has `reason` (MATCH | GUEST_WIN | GUEST_LOSS | TOURNAMENT_BONUS | DECAY), `delta`, `ratingAfter`, and optional `matchId`/`tournamentId`. Optional `limit` defaults to 50 and is clamped to 200. Empty when the user has no linked player; 404 when the user does not exist.

item.name: Get Public Profile Calendar
item.description: Returns a public profile mini-calendar combining scheduled/live/completed/walkover matches with PUBLIC trainings for the selected local-date range. `timezone` defaults to UTC when omitted.

item.name: Get My Profile Calendar
item.description: Returns the authenticated user's profile mini-calendar combining all of their match events and both PUBLIC/PRIVATE trainings for the selected local-date range.

request.description: Authenticated read of tournaments owned by or joined by the current user.

item.name: Get Public Trainings By Date Range
item.description: Returns only PUBLIC training sessions for the selected user within the requested date range, plus compact calendar day counts for the UI.

item.name: Get My Trainings By Date Range
item.description: Returns the authenticated user's training history for a selected date range, including both PUBLIC and PRIVATE sessions plus compact calendar day counts for the UI.

item.name: Create Training
item.description: Creates a new training entry for the authenticated user. Visibility is required and controls whether the session is visible on public profiles.

item.name: Update Training
item.description: Updates the selected training entry for the authenticated user. Provide at least one field; `visibility` can be changed per session.

item.name: Delete Training
item.description: Deletes the selected training entry for the authenticated user.

item.name: Get My Rackets
item.description: Returns the authenticated user's non-deleted rackets, including private ones.

item.name: Delete Racket
item.description: Soft-deletes the racket and hides its history from normal reads.

item.name: Delete Racket Stringing
item.description: Soft-deletes a single stringing entry.

item.name: Get Public Rackets By User
item.description: Returns only public, non-deleted rackets for the selected user.

item.name: Create Club
item.description: Platform admin only. Clubs are provisioned manually by the platform operator on behalf of the club's user (`ownerUserId`, resolved via Get Users / Get User By Username). Regular users get 403; unknown ownerUserId gets 404. Bootstrap the first platform admin via SQL: UPDATE users SET role = 'PLATFORM_ADMIN' WHERE auth_subject = '<clerk user sub>';

item.name: Delete Club
item.description: Platform admin only. Club owners/admins can no longer delete their club.

item.name: Send Club Contact Request
item.description: Public (anonymous) onboarding form for clubs that want to join. Records the inquiry; the operator reviews it and provisions the club manually via Create Club. clubName, contactName and email are required; phone and message optional (message max 4000 chars).

body.raw:
{
  "clubName": "Club de Tenis Ribera",
  "contactName": "Ana García",
  "email": "ana@clubribera.com",
  "phone": "+34 600 000 000",
  "message": "Queremos publicar nuestros torneos."
}

item.name: Get Club Contact Requests
item.description: Platform admin only. Lists club onboarding inquiries, newest first — the operator's review queue before provisioning clubs.

item.name: Delete Club Contact Request
item.description: Platform admin only. Clears a handled inquiry (accept flow: read request, create the club, then delete the request). 404 on unknown id.

request.description: Provide clubId for a public club-hosted tournament. Omit clubId for a private owner-hosted tournament.
Create a knockout phase using the current phase configuration contract.
Create a single round-robin group phase. Each player faces every other player in the same group once.
Create a Swiss phase and omit advancingCount. The backend will default advancingCount to the full player count, so every player advances to the next phase.
Create a Swiss phase with an explicit advancingCount. Only the top ranked players advance to the next phase.

item.name: Add Players To Tournament
item.description: Manager-only direct add. If the player has a pending join request for this tournament, the request is marked ACCEPTED.

item.name: Create Tournament Join Request
item.description: Authenticated player requests to join a DRAFT tournament. If the user has no player profile, one is created automatically from playerName or the user's username.

item.name: Get Tournament Join Requests
item.description: Manager-only request queue. Optional status filter: PENDING, ACCEPTED, REJECTED, WITHDRAWN, EXPIRED.

item.name: Get My Tournament Join Requests
item.description: Authenticated player's own join request history. Optional status filter: PENDING, ACCEPTED, REJECTED, WITHDRAWN, EXPIRED.

item.name: Withdraw Tournament Join Request
item.description: Authenticated requester withdraws a PENDING join request.

item.name: Accept Tournament Join Request
item.description: Manager accepts a PENDING request, optionally assigning a seed, and adds the player to tournament_players.

item.name: Reject Tournament Join Request
item.description: Manager rejects a PENDING request. Rejected players have a 7-day resubmission cooldown unless a manager unlocks resubmission.

item.name: Allow Join Request Resubmit
item.description: Manager clears a rejected request's cooldown so the player can request again immediately.

item.name: Reset Tournament
item.description: Resets a STARTED or COMPLETED tournament back to DRAFT. Generated matches, dependencies, groups, Swiss rankings, champion, and tournament rating events are cleared so players/phases can be edited again.

request.description: Authenticated read. Returns private tournaments owned by or joined by the current user plus public tournaments they are registered in.
Authenticated. Joins a private tournament by invite code and auto-registers the caller as a player. Bad code returns 404; duplicate registration returns 409.
Authenticated. Omit clubId to create an owner-owned PRIVATE tournament with inviteCode. Private tournaments are hidden from public listings and do not affect public Elo.

item.name: Scenarios
item.description: Runnable collection-runner scenarios. `Complex Tournament - Initialize` starts an end-to-end zero-state flow that generates a test JWT, creates the club owner and club implicitly, creates a GROUP -> SWISS -> KNOCKOUT tournament, adds 8 players, scores each scheduled match, and stops when the tournament reaches COMPLETED. `Swiss Knockout With Bye - Initialize` does the same for a `SWISS -> KNOCKOUT` flow with 5 players so round 1 includes a bye and Swiss advances the top 4. This assumes the backend is running with the test JWT settings from `AuthConfig` (`issuer=http://localhost/test-issuer`, `audience=test-audience`, `secret=test-secret`) or equivalent environment overrides.

item.name: Complex Tournament Scenario
item.description: Zero-state GROUP -> SWISS -> KNOCKOUT scenario. Starts with test-token auth bootstrapping, creates its own club and tournament, and runs the tournament to COMPLETED.

request.description: Initializes collection variables and generates a test JWT for the complex tournament flow. Run this request in the Collection Runner to kick off the full zero-state scenario.

Creates the owner club. The first authenticated request auto-creates the local user from the JWT subject. NOTE: POST /clubs now requires PLATFORM_ADMIN — on a zero-state DB the first run 403s after auto-creating the user (id 1); promote it (UPDATE users SET role = 'PLATFORM_ADMIN' WHERE id = 1;) and re-run the scenario.
Creates the tournament shell for the multi-phase scenario using the scenario club created earlier in the run.
Phase 1 is a single round-robin group stage with 2 groups of 4 players and the top 2 from each group advancing.
Phase 2 is Swiss with 4 advancers, so every player who reaches Swiss also advances into the knockout phase after Swiss standings are resolved.
Phase 3 is a knockout bracket for the 4 Swiss advancers.
Adds 8 external players so the group stage fills exactly 2 groups of 4.
Starts the tournament. The first generated matches should belong to the group phase.
Loads all tournament matches, finds the next scheduled match in deterministic order, and loops until no scheduled matches remain.

exec: pm.test("Next scheduled match is ready to score", function () {

request.description: Scores the scheduled match selected by the previous request using a deterministic straight-sets win for player1.
Confirms that the tournament reached COMPLETED after the scoring loop finished.
Fetches the final bracket and verifies that all three configured phases were played.

exec: pm.test("Final bracket includes all configured phases", function () {

item.name: Swiss Knockout With Bye Scenario
item.description: Zero-state SWISS -> KNOCKOUT scenario with 5 players, round-1 bye coverage, explicit advancingCount, and full tournament completion checks.

request.description: Initializes collection variables and generates a test JWT for the zero-state Swiss plus knockout scenario with an odd player count.

exec: postman.setNextRequest("Swiss Knockout With Bye - Create Club");

item.name: Swiss Knockout With Bye - Create Club

request.description: Creates the owner club for the zero-state Swiss plus knockout scenario. NOTE: POST /clubs now requires PLATFORM_ADMIN — on a zero-state DB the first run 403s after auto-creating the user (id 1); promote it (UPDATE users SET role = 'PLATFORM_ADMIN' WHERE id = 1;) and re-run the scenario.

exec: postman.setNextRequest("Swiss Knockout With Bye - Create Tournament");

item.name: Swiss Knockout With Bye - Create Tournament

request.description: Creates the tournament shell for the Swiss plus knockout scenario.

exec: postman.setNextRequest("Swiss Knockout With Bye - Create Phase 1 (Swiss)");

item.name: Swiss Knockout With Bye - Create Phase 1 (Swiss)

request.description: Phase 1 is Swiss with 5 players and `advancingCount = 4`, which should create a round 1 bye and send the top 4 to knockout.

exec: postman.setNextRequest("Swiss Knockout With Bye - Create Phase 2 (Knockout)");

item.name: Swiss Knockout With Bye - Create Phase 2 (Knockout)

request.description: Phase 2 is a knockout bracket for the top 4 Swiss players.

exec: postman.setNextRequest("Swiss Knockout With Bye - Add Players");

item.name: Swiss Knockout With Bye - Add Players

request.description: Adds 5 external players to force a Swiss round 1 bye.
Starts the tournament and verifies that the Swiss phase was created with at least one walkover in round 1.

exec: postman.setNextRequest("Swiss Knockout With Bye - Load Next Match");

item.name: Swiss Knockout With Bye - Load Next Match

request.description: Loads all tournament matches, finds the next scheduled match in deterministic order, and loops until the Swiss plus knockout flow is fully completed.

exec: postman.setNextRequest("Swiss Knockout With Bye - Verify Completion");
pm.test("Next scheduled match is ready to score", function () {
postman.setNextRequest("Swiss Knockout With Bye - Score Next Match");

item.name: Swiss Knockout With Bye - Score Next Match

request.description: Scores the selected scheduled match with a deterministic straight-sets win for player1.

exec: postman.setNextRequest("Swiss Knockout With Bye - Load Next Match");

item.name: Swiss Knockout With Bye - Verify Completion

request.description: Confirms that the tournament reached COMPLETED after Swiss and knockout progression finished.

exec: postman.setNextRequest("Swiss Knockout With Bye - Verify Bracket");

item.name: Swiss Knockout With Bye - Verify Bracket

request.description: Fetches the final bracket and verifies that Swiss and knockout phases both ran and that the Swiss phase contains a walkover.

exec: pm.test("Final bracket includes Swiss and knockout phases", function () {

request.description: Authenticated. Creates a private league, generates inviteCode, and adds the owner as a member.
Authenticated. Returns leagues owned by or joined by the current user.
Authenticated. Joins a league by invite code.
Authenticated. League owner or member only; non-members receive 404.
Owner only.
Owner only.
Owner only. Returns the league with a new inviteCode.
Owner or member only. Members include league-scoped rating, ratedMatches, wins, and losses.
Owner only. Resolves email server-side; unknown email returns 404.
Owner only. Members with recorded matches cannot be removed.
Owner or member only.
Owner or match participant. Replays league-scoped Elo immediately; public ratings are untouched.
Owner only. Replays league-scoped Elo after deletion.
